properties.name: Bill's Bay & Skeleton Beach Reef Walks
properties.comment: Walk the shallow northern limestone rocky flats at low tide. A legendary natural collector trap for bright orange cockles, patterned cone shells, and glossy cowries washed up from the fringing reef.

properties.name: Coalseam Riverbed Scramble
properties.location: Coalseam Conservation Park, Irwin River Bank
properties.comment: Walk right down onto the dry rocky riverbank. Flip over individual river stones to find 250-million-year-old brachiopod and marine bivalve imprints. Leave your finds on the bank for the next traveller!

properties.name: Eyre Highway Limestone Road-Cuts
properties.location: Nullarbor Plain, between Balladonia and Madura
properties.comment: Pull into major highway rest areas and inspect the rubble piles blasted from vertical limestone road-cut walls. Hundreds of sea shell casts, fossilised echinoids and bryozoans sit loose on the surface. Miner's Right required to collect.

properties.name: Fossil Picnic Area — Coalseam
properties.location: Coalseam Conservation Park, Irwin River Bank
properties.comment: Walk down onto the dry riverbed next to the picnic tables. Thousands of 250-million-year-old fossilised marine shells exposed across the surface of the stones.

properties.name: Flinders Bay Shell Grit Beds
properties.comment: Where the Indian and Southern Oceans collide. Rocky corners trap heavy shell grit layers after winter storms. Rock pools and weed lines yield large robust cold-water sea shells and tumbled granite pebbles.

properties.name: Gantheaume Beach Low Tide Pools
properties.comment: Massive tidal changes expose complex rock pools along the red pindan cliffs. Sandy pockets inside low-tide rock pools reveal tropical species like snakehead and ring-top cowries.

properties.name: Gantheaume Point Low Tide Reef Walk
properties.comment: When the tide drops below 2.1 metres, climb onto the flat red sandstone shelf to search for 130-million-year-old dinosaur footprints. Excellent interpretive signs show you what shapes to look for.

properties.name: Gascoyne River Gravel Bars
properties.location: Gascoyne Junction area, dry riverbed sections
properties.comment: Walk the wide dry pebble and gravel curves of the riverbed. Flip over dark heavy siltstones to find detailed marine imprints including crinoid stems, spiriferid brachiopods and solitary corals. Miner's Right required.

properties.name: Gingin Chalk Exposure Areas
properties.comment: Famous zone for fossilised marine life and shark teeth. Requires contacting local managers or landowners before accessing the chalk faces. Miner's Right and landowner consent needed.

properties.name: Green Head Beachcombing Ledger
properties.comment: One specific bay gets choked with trillions of tiny pristine pure-white shells. Collectors nickname them 'white rice' — almost identical in size and perfectly preserved.

properties.name: Hamelin Pool Stromatolites
properties.comment: Active living fossils representing life on Earth from 3.5 billion years ago. Short walk to the old quarry and shoreline to see them in the shallow brine. One of the most significant fossil sites on Earth.

properties.name: Hopetoun Back Beach Shell Sifting
properties.comment: Ocean currents dump a massive concentration of shells here. Sifting the shell grit reveals an exceptionally high percentage of deep pink, red, and chocolate-caramel whole shells.

properties.name: Minim Cove Fossil Bank
properties.location: Mosman Park, Perth Metro — Swan River
properties.comment: Secret hands-on spot right in Perth's suburbs. Run your hands along rocky limestone cliffs at the river's edge to spot thousands of ancient prehistoric bivalves, gastropods, and fossilised weevil cocoons.

properties.name: Norseman Public Fossicking Area
properties.location: Norseman Region, 10 km west of townsite
properties.type: Ancient Geologic Fossicking — Agate and Chert
properties.comment: The only official legal public fossicking area on a roadside layout in WA. Walk the open red dirt, kick over stones, search the gravel — and you are allowed to keep what you find here!

properties.name: Point Peron Rock & Shell Search
properties.comment: Walk rugged honeycombed limestone headlands and sandy coves. Known hotspot for beach-polished sea glass, iridescent shells, and fossilised plant root tracks in the ancient limestone.

properties.name: Shell Beach Conservation Park
properties.comment: One of only two beaches in the world made 100% from shells. Trillions of tiny white Hamelin Cockle shells go up to 10 metres deep. Walk and read the educational signs about why they thrive here.

properties.name: Turquoise Bay Drift Shoreline
properties.comment: Heavy drift current pushes unique deep-sea treasures onto the tide lines. Search for olive shells, heavy-spined murex, and moon snails. Ningaloo is a marine park — look, photograph, and leave them for the ecosystem.

properties.name: Windjana Gorge Ancient Coral Reef
properties.comment: The towering canyon walls are a 350-million-year-old barrier reef. Look along the vertical rock face near the entry gate for fossilised extinct corals and sponges frozen in the dark grey stone.

properties.name: Eighty Mile Beach
properties.comment: Widely regarded as one of the best shell collecting beaches in the world. Massive 9-metre tides clear the sand flats twice daily, leaving thousands of pristine giant shells. Famous for intact Mother-of-Pearl, sand dollars, and massive bailer shells.
